evals.prompt: Execute the goals in Docs/Goals/. Master.md lists one pending goal: Docs/Goals/combat/add-parry-mechanic.md with title '[Combat] Add parry mechanic' (priority: high, no dependencies). The project is a Unity project with existing spec at Docs/Specs/Combat.md. The repo is on branch 'main' with a clean working tree.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads Master.md, identifies the single pending goal, creates a git worktree with branch goal/combat-add-parry, spawns a subagent in that worktree with full goal text + spec content. The subagent implements, verifies (three gates), commits, pushes, and creates a PR. The controller verifies the PR, marks the goal complete, delegates a spec update, and cleans up the worktree.
expectations: Reads Docs/Goals/Master.md first as the source of truth for goals
Creates a git worktree with a goal/ prefixed branch (e.g., goal/combat-add-parry)
Worktree is created outside the repo root (e.g., ../.worktrees/)
Spawns a subagent with run_in_background=true for the goal
Subagent prompt includes the full goal text (objective, criteria, constraints)
Subagent prompt includes the Docs/Specs/Combat.md spec content as architectural blueprint
Subagent prompt includes the worktree path and branch name
Subagent performs three-gate verification (static analysis, domain check, spec compliance)
Subagent commits changes and pushes the branch
Subagent creates a PR via gh pr create with --base main --head goal/combat-add-parry
Controller delegates a spec update after goal completes
Controller cleans up the worktree after PR is confirmed
Execution summary includes Pull Requests Created section with PR URL

evals.prompt: Execute goals. Master.md lists three pending goals with no dependencies between them: '[Combat] Add parry' (high), '[UI] Build health bar' (high), '[Audio] Add footstep sounds' (medium). The project is Unity. All three can execute in parallel (Wave 1). The repo is on branch 'main'.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads Master.md, identifies all three goals as independent (no depends_on), groups them into Wave 1. Creates three git worktrees in parallel with branches goal/combat-add-parry, goal/ui-build-health-bar, goal/audio-add-footstep-sounds. Spawns three subagents with run_in_background=true simultaneously. Each works independently. Controller collects results as agents complete, verifies PRs, updates Master.md, cleans up worktrees.
expectations: Reads Master.md and identifies all three goals as independent (Wave 1)
Creates three separate git worktrees — one per goal
Each worktree has a unique goal/-prefixed branch
Spawns three subagents in parallel (all with run_in_background=true)
No sequential waiting — all agents launched in the same turn
Each subagent receives its own worktree path and branch
Controller processes completion notifications as they arrive
Each goal gets its own PR created back to main
Master.md is updated with completed status for finished goals
Worktrees are cleaned up after PRs are confirmed

evals.prompt: Execute goals. Master.md lists: '[Core] Add event system' (high, no deps) and '[Combat] Add parry mechanic' (high, depends_on: '[Core] Add event system'). The project is Unity. The second goal cannot start until the first completes.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads Master.md, builds a dependency graph. Wave 1 contains only '[Core] Add event system'. Wave 2 contains '[Combat] Add parry mechanic'. Creates worktree for Wave 1, spawns subagent. After Wave 1 completes and PR is created, creates worktree for Wave 2 branching from the updated base (or the Wave 1 branch), spawns second subagent. Sequential wave execution respecting dependencies.
expectations: Reads Master.md and builds a dependency DAG
Identifies Wave 1: [Core] Add event system (no deps)
Identifies Wave 2: [Combat] Add parry mechanic (depends on Core)
Creates worktree and spawns subagent for Wave 1 only
Does NOT create Wave 2 worktree until Wave 1 completes
After Wave 1 PR is created, proceeds to Wave 2
Wave 2 worktree branches from updated base to include Wave 1 changes
Each wave gets its own worktree and subagent
Execution summary shows waves executed and dependency ordering

evals.prompt: Execute goals. Master.md lists one pending goal: '[Crafting] Implement recipe system' (high). No spec exists in Docs/Specs/ for Crafting. The project is Unity. Repo on branch 'main'.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads Master.md, creates worktree, spawns subagent. The subagent is told no spec exists. After the goal completes and PR is created, the controller delegates a spec CREATION (Feature Spec mode, not Update) via unity-spec to create Docs/Specs/Crafting.md from the implementation.
expectations: Checks Docs/Specs/ for Crafting spec and correctly determines none exists
Subagent prompt says no spec exists — implement from acceptance criteria
After goal completes, delegates spec creation using Feature Spec mode (not Update)
Creates a worktree with goal/crafting-implement-recipe-system branch
Subagent creates PR after implementation
Spec creation results in new Docs/Specs/Crafting.md

evals.prompt: Execute goals. Master.md lists one pending goal but the subagent reports BLOCKED because the goal requires an API key that isn't configured. The controller must handle the blocked status.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates worktree, spawns subagent. When the subagent reports BLOCKED, the controller reads the blocker reason, marks the goal as blocked in Master.md and the goal file, cleans up the worktree, and reports the blocked goal with reason in the execution summary.
expectations: Creates worktree and spawns subagent normally
When subagent reports BLOCKED, does not retry endlessly
Marks goal status as blocked in the goal file frontmatter
Updates Master.md with blocked status
Cleans up the worktree despite the goal being blocked
Execution summary reports the blocked goal with the blocker reason
